event: African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) agreement signed in Kigali by 44 African Union member states
impact: Established framework for continent-wide free trade zone; Nigeria initially delayed signing until July 2019

event: Nigeria closes all land borders to goods trade, citing smuggling and security concerns
impact: Severe disruption to cross-border trade; Benin and Niger economies hit hardest as transit trade through Lagos-Cotonou and Kano-Niamey corridors halted; smuggling through informal channels increased

event: Nigeria signs the AfCFTA agreement at AU Extraordinary Summit in Niamey
impact: Africa's largest economy joins the continental free trade area, completing ECOWAS participation; ratification and implementation timelines set

event: Military coup in Mali; President Ibrahim Boubacar Keita overthrown by Colonel Assimi Goita
impact: ECOWAS imposed initial sanctions and demanded civilian transition; disrupted Bamako-Dakar and Bamako-Abidjan trade corridors; transitional government established

event: Nigeria partially reopens land borders after 16-month closure
impact: Gradual resumption of formal cross-border trade; Cotonou port transit volumes began recovering; Nigeria-Niger trade routes slowly reactivated

event: African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) officially enters into force; trading begins under AfCFTA framework
impact: Tariff liberalization begins on 90% of goods; rules of origin negotiations ongoing; implementation uneven across member states but creates legal framework for intra-African trade expansion

event: ECOWAS Common External Tariff (CET) Phase III implementation begins across member states
impact: Harmonized 5-band tariff structure (0%, 5%, 10%, 20%, 35%) applied region-wide; reduces intra-ECOWAS trade barriers; implementation compliance varies by country

event: Second military coup in Mali; Colonel Goita deposes transitional President Ndaw and PM Ouane
impact: ECOWAS suspends Mali and imposes targeted sanctions; further isolation of Bamako from regional trade networks; FDI flows to Mali sharply decline

event: Military coup in Guinea; Colonel Mamady Doumbouya overthrows President Alpha Conde
impact: ECOWAS suspends Guinea; disrupts bauxite and mining sector FDI; Conakry port operations initially impacted; Guinea's mining exports (critical for aluminum supply chain) continue under junta

event: ECOWAS imposes severe economic and financial sanctions on Mali including border closures and trade embargo
impact: Landlocked Mali cut off from regional trade; Bamako-Dakar rail corridor frozen; Sikasso-Bouake trade halted; severe humanitarian impact; Mali turns to Russia and non-ECOWAS partners

event: Military coup in Burkina Faso; Lt. Col. Paul-Henri Damiba overthrows President Roch Marc Christian Kabore
impact: ECOWAS suspends Burkina Faso; Ouagadougou and Bobo-Dioulasso trade hubs disrupted; landlocked country faces trade rerouting challenges; regional security concerns intensify across Sahel

event: ECOWAS lifts most economic sanctions on Mali while maintaining political suspension and targeted sanctions
impact: Partial trade resumption through Dakar and Abidjan corridors; Mali remains politically suspended; junta extends transition timeline; trade volumes recover partially but FDI remains depressed

event: Second coup in Burkina Faso; Captain Ibrahim Traore deposes Lt. Col. Damiba
impact: Deepens Burkina Faso's political isolation; accelerates pivot toward Russia and away from France/ECOWAS; Sahel security belt of coup-led states solidifies

event: Military coup in Niger; General Abdourahamane Tchiani overthrows President Mohamed Bazoum
impact: ECOWAS imposes severe sanctions, closes borders, threatens military intervention; Nigeria cuts electricity supply to Niger; Niamey-Kano and Niamey-Cotonou trade corridors disrupted; fourth Sahel coup in three years

event: Alliance of Sahel States (AES) formed by Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger with mutual defense pact
impact: Creates formal counter-bloc to ECOWAS; signals potential permanent fracture of West African integration; three landlocked states pivot toward Russia, Turkey, and non-Western partners; ECOWAS coherence severely tested

event: Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger jointly announce immediate withdrawal from ECOWAS
impact: Unprecedented rupture in West African integration; ECOWAS treaty requires one-year withdrawal notice; creates uncertainty for CFA franc zone membership, trade agreements, and free movement protocols; 72 million people potentially affected

event: ECOWAS lifts economic sanctions on Niger while maintaining political suspension
impact: Partial de-escalation; Nigeria-Niger border trade gradually resumes; Benin transit corridor reopens; ECOWAS signals pragmatic engagement despite ongoing political disagreements

event: Abidjan-Lagos Highway Corridor project secures additional AfDB and EU financing; Phase 1 construction advances
impact: 1,028 km coastal highway connecting five countries; will reduce Lagos-Abidjan transit time from 3 days to under 15 hours; transforms trade capacity along most economically active West African corridor

event: Dakar-Bamako railway rehabilitation project receives renewed study funding despite political tensions
impact: 1,228 km railway critical for landlocked Mali's access to Dakar port; rehabilitation stalled by Mali's political situation but remains strategically important; Chinese and Turkish investors express interest

event: ECOWAS one-year withdrawal notice period expires for Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger; ECOWAS extends transition period to July 2025
impact: ECOWAS attempts to negotiate continued membership; AES states maintain withdrawal stance; free movement protocols and trade preferences hang in balance; regional economic integration faces most serious crisis since founding

event: ECOWAS Eco single currency project postponed again; revised target set for 2027
impact: Non-CFA ECOWAS members fail to meet convergence criteria (inflation, debt, deficit targets); Nigeria and Ghana's high inflation rates remain primary obstacles; monetary integration timeline pushed back further
